heading: Imposition of tax on indoor tanning services
label: 5000B Imposition of tax on indoor tanning services

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: There is hereby imposed on any indoor tanning service a tax equal to 10 percent of the amount paid for such service (determined without regard to this section), whether paid by insurance or otherwise.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “indoor tanning service” means a service employing any electronic product designed to incorporate 1 or more ultraviolet lamps and intended for the irradiation of an individual by ultraviolet radiation, with wavelengths in air between 200 and 400 nanometers, to induce skin tanning.

children.heading: Exclusion of phototherapy services
children.label: 2 Exclusion of phototherapy services
children.text: Such term does not include any phototherapy service performed by a licensed medical professional.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The tax imposed by this section shall be paid by the individual on whom the service is performed.

children.heading: Collection
children.label: 2 Collection
children.text: Every person receiving a payment for services on which a tax is imposed under subsection (a) shall collect the amount of the tax from the individual on whom the service is performed and remit such tax quarterly to the Secretary at such time and in such manner as provided by the Secretary.

children.heading: Secondary liability
children.label: 3 Secondary liability
children.text: Where any tax imposed by subsection (a) is not paid at the time payments for indoor tanning services are made, then to the extent that such tax is not collected, such tax shall be paid by the person who performs the service.
